instruction: Read each passage carefully and answer the questions that follow. Answers should be based only on the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Bholi’s real name was Sulekha. She was the youngest daughter of Ramlal. As a child, she fell from a cot and suffered damage to her brain. Later, she had smallpox which left permanent marks on her face. Because of these reasons, people considered her dull and neglected her.
questions: What was Bholi’s real name?
Who was Bholi’s father?
What accident happened to Bholi in childhood?
Which illness disfigured Bholi’s face?
Why was Bholi neglected by others?

passages.paragraph: Bholi also had a stammering problem which made it difficult for her to speak clearly. Whenever she tried to speak, people laughed at her. This made her feel afraid and silent. Gradually, she lost confidence and stopped speaking freely.
questions: What problem did Bholi have while speaking?
How did people react when Bholi spoke?
What effect did people’s reaction have on her?
Why did Bholi become silent?
What happened to Bholi’s confidence?

passages.paragraph: When Bholi was seven years old, her parents decided to send her to school. She was frightened because she did not know what school meant. She thought she was being taken away forever like her sisters who had gone to their husbands’ homes.
questions: At what age was Bholi sent to school?
Why was Bholi frightened to go to school?
What misunderstanding did Bholi have about school?
Why did she think she was being taken away?
Who had already left home before Bholi?

passages.paragraph: At school, Bholi was bathed, dressed in clean clothes, and given a book. The school environment was different from home. She felt that school was a better place because she was treated with care and kindness.
questions: What preparations were made for Bholi at school?
How was the school environment different from home?
Why did Bholi feel happy at school?
What did she receive at school?
How did school make Bholi feel?

passages.paragraph: The teacher spoke to Bholi gently and encouraged her to speak. She told Bholi that she could learn and would become confident one day. These kind words had a deep impact on Bholi’s mind.
questions: How did the teacher speak to Bholi?
What did the teacher encourage Bholi to do?
What promise did the teacher make to Bholi?
How did the teacher’s words affect Bholi?
Who played a key role in building Bholi’s confidence?

passages.paragraph: As time passed, education changed Bholi completely. She learned to speak clearly and gained confidence. Education helped her understand her self-worth and removed her fear.
questions: What brought change in Bholi’s life?
What improvement was seen in her speech?
What did Bholi gain through education?
How did education affect her fear?
What did Bholi learn about herself?

passages.paragraph: When Bholi grew up, her parents arranged her marriage with Bishamber Nath, a wealthy grocer. He was much older than Bholi and demanded dowry at the wedding ceremony.
questions: Who was Bholi’s bridegroom?
What was Bishamber Nath’s profession?
How was he different in age from Bholi?
What demand did he make at the wedding?
Who arranged Bholi’s marriage?

passages.paragraph: At first, Bholi agreed to the marriage to protect her parents’ honour. However, when the groom demanded dowry, she realised that accepting the marriage would mean losing her self-respect.
questions: Why did Bholi initially agree to the marriage?
What made her rethink her decision?
What demand shocked Bholi?
What did Bholi realise about the marriage?
Which value became important to Bholi at this moment?

passages.paragraph: Bholi boldly refused to marry Bishamber Nath. She spoke clearly and confidently in front of everyone. This showed her transformation from a fearful girl into a confident woman.
questions: What decision did Bholi take at the wedding?
How did Bholi speak at that moment?
What quality did Bholi show?
What change is seen in Bholi’s personality?
Whom did Bholi refuse to marry?

passages.paragraph: At the end of the story, Bholi decides to serve her parents and become a teacher. She proves that education and self-respect are more important than marriage. Her journey becomes a message of women empowerment.
questions: What decision does Bholi take at the end?
Whom does Bholi decide to serve?
What profession does she choose?
What does Bholi value more than marriage?
What message does Bholi’s journey give?
